commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces seven MSTeams graph-upload success-path `res.json()` calls with the shared `readProviderJsonResponse` 16 MiB capped reader.

PR surface: Source +1. Total +1 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main injects `fetchFn` through the affected graph-upload helpers and then calls raw `res.json()` on successful responses. A streaming JSON response larger than 16 MiB would exercise the unbounded path; I did not run it because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The patch has no discrete code defect for an automated repair lane, but the PR still needs contributor or maintainer proof/test validation before merge.

target.title: fix(msteams): bound graph-upload JSON response reads to prevent OOM
target.body:
## Summary
Replace 7 unbounded res.json() calls with readProviderJsonResponse (16 MiB cap) in MSTeams OneDrive/SharePoint upload, sharing link, driveItem properties, chat listing, and chat member fetch paths.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: 7 unbounded res.json() calls replaced with readProviderJsonResponse (16 MiB cap).

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 22, OpenClaw main @ 87db23e543

**Proof evidence**:
```
oneDriveUpload: chunks=17 cancelled=true PASS
createSharingLink: chunks=17 cancelled=true PASS
sharePointUpload: chunks=17 cancelled=true PASS
getDriveItem: chunks=17 cancelled=true PASS
listChats: chunks=17 cancelled=true PASS
getChatMembers: chunks=17 cancelled=true PASS
createSharePointSharingLink: chunks=17 cancelled=true PASS

PASS: all 7 graph-upload paths bounded at 16 MiB
```

**All existing tests pass**: 11/11 in graph-upload.test.ts.

## Risk
Low. Same readProviderJsonResponse helper already used in msteams bot-framework.ts and graph.ts.
target.author: cxbasdev
